title: 10 medicinal constituents of Elaeagnus angustifolia (silverberry)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Elaeagnus angustifolia flavonoids is a class of compounds found in its fruits, leaves, and stems. These flavonoids have been reported to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. They have also been shown to exhibit cytotoxic effects against certain cancer cell lines, making them potential leads for the development of new cancer therapeutic agents.

Elaeagnus angustifolia phenolic acids is a type of compound found in its leaves and stems. These compounds are responsible for the plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. They have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects, making them potential candidates for treating various diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders. Elaeagnus angustifolia phenolic acids also possess strong free radical scavenging activity, which helps protect cells from oxidative damage.

Elaeagnus angustifolia ellagic acid is a potent polyphenol compound found in its leaves and fruits. This phytochemical has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimutagenic properties. Ellagic acid has also shown promise in inhibiting the growth of certain cancer cells and viruses, making it a promising area of research in the fields of medicine and nutrition. Its unique chemical structure provides a strong foundation for further investigation into its therapeutic applications.

Elaeagnus angustifolia quercetin is a flavonoid glycoside compound isolated from its leaves and stems. This bioactive compound has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties. Quercetin in Elaeagnus angustifolia has been found to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, reduce oxidative stress, and modulate immune responses, making it a promising natural product for the prevention and treatment of various diseases.

Elaeagnus angustifolia kaempferol is a type of flavonoid glycoside that has been isolated from the fruit of Elaeagnus angustifolia. It is a naturally occurring compound that exhibits various biological activities, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Kaempferol has been shown to have potential benefits for cardiovascular health, as well as anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects. Its chemical structure is characterized by a flavonoid skeleton with a kaempferol aglycone core.

Elaeagnus angustifolia gallic acid is a key component of its leaves and stems. This phenolic compound has been extensively studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Gallic acid has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of various diseases. Its unique chemical structure allows it to interact with biomolecules, rendering it an attractive molecule for further research and development.

Elaeagnus angustifolia protocatechuic acid is a type of phenolic compound found in its leaves and stems. It has been isolated and studied for its potential bioactive properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In vitro studies have shown that protocatechuic acid exhibits inhibitory effects on certain enzymes and free radicals, suggesting its potential as a natural therapeutic agent for various diseases.

Elaeagnus angustifolia vanillic acid is a phenolic compound naturally found in its leaves and stems. This antioxidant-rich acid has been shown to possess various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties. Vanillic acid has also been found to have potential therapeutic applications, such as treating cardiovascular diseases and neurodegenerative disorders, making it a promising area of research in the fields of medicine and pharmacology.

Elaeagnus angustifolia catechin is a type of flavonoid glycoside found in its leaves and fruits. It has been shown to possess various bioactive properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, it has been reported to exhibit potential therapeutic effects on cardiovascular disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders. The compound is also known for its ability to protect against oxidative stress and DNA damage.

Elaeagnus angustifolia epicatechin is a polyphenolic compound found in its leaves and stems. It has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Epicatechin has been shown to have cardiovascular protective effects by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Additionally, it may help to prevent cancer cell growth and improve insulin sensitivity, making it a promising natural compound for overall health promotion.

intro: Elaeagnus angustifolia has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These compounds impart medicinal properties like antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The antioxidants can help protect against chronic diseases, while the anti-inflammatory properties may alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other conditions. Additionally, the antimicrobial effects can support wound healing and prevent infections. For instance, the flavonoids in silverberry have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, ultimately benefiting overall well-being and quality of life.
intro_desc: Elaeagnus angustifolia has active constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. These properties contribute to its traditional use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. For instance, its flavonoids can improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood lipid profiles, while its antimicrobial properties can aid in wound healing by preventing infection.
